skill.name: specdd-do
skill.description: Implements code, documentation, tests, tasks, or spec changes inside an existing SpecDD project with explicit write authority.
skill.summary: Apply SpecDD project rules before changing code, documentation, tests, or tasks.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and ordinary text as weak cryptography. Review of SKILL.md found no executable code, no network behavior, no credential access, and no prompt injection attempt. The skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need implementation work that respects active project contracts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through authority checks, scoped edits, validation, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Checks that a project has a root .specdd/bootstrap.md before using the workflow.
Reads bootstrap files in the defined SpecDD order when context is not reliable.
Resolves governing specs for target files, tasks, or behaviors before planning edits.
Requires clear write authority from the nearest applicable local spec before editing.
Guides implementation toward small scoped changes followed by relevant checks.
Reports specs used, files changed, verification results, and remaining uncertainty.
limitations: It applies only to projects that already use SpecDD.
It does not create a new SpecDD setup or adopt SpecDD for a project.
It cannot override project specs, public contracts, or security constraints.
It requires available project files and relevant validation tools to complete checks.

use_cases.title: Implement a Scoped Code Change
use_cases.description: Apply a requested code change only after resolving the governing SpecDD chain and write authority.

use_cases.title: Update Tests Under Project Rules
use_cases.description: Modify or add tests while following local specifications and running relevant validation commands.

use_cases.title: Revise Documentation With Authority
use_cases.description: Edit documentation only where the active specs allow the change and report the governing sources.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Simple SpecDD Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small implementation task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-do to update the requested file. Resolve the active SpecDD authority first, make the smallest correct change, run relevant checks, and report the specs used.

prompt_templates.title: Change Tests for a Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request involving tests and validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-do to update the tests for this feature. Identify the target path, read the governing specs, confirm write authority, implement the change, and run the relevant test command.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Multi-File Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request where several files may be governed by different specs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-do for this task across the affected files. Resolve each effective spec chain, confirm authority for every edit, keep the change scoped, and summarize verification for each area.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a Possible Contract Conflict
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request where the requested change may violate a spec.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-do to evaluate this requested change against the governing specs. If any Must not, Forbids, write authority, security, destructive behavior, or public contract rule blocks the work, stop and explain the conflict.

output_examples.input: Update a handler in an existing SpecDD project.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the bootstrap chain, reads the relevant local spec, confirms write authority, edits the handler, runs the targeted test, and reports the governing specs.

output_examples.input: Modify documentation for a module with local specs.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the documentation authority, makes only the approved wording change, validates formatting when available, and lists remaining uncertainty.

output_examples.input: Request a change that conflicts with a Must not rule.
output_examples.output: The assistant stops before editing, explains the specific governing rule that blocks the request, and asks for revised authority or a different target.

best_practices: Start with a clear target path, behavior, task, or changed file list.
Keep implementation narrow and tied to the nearest governing spec.
Run the most relevant available checks before reporting completion.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill in a project without a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Do not infer write authority from nearby filenames or similar modules.
Do not weaken specs or complete unrelated work while implementing a request.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It guides implementation work inside existing SpecDD projects while respecting bootstrap files, governing specs, and write authority.

faq.question: Can it create a new SpecDD project?
faq.answer: No. It is only for projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to run relevant checks when they are available and appropriate for the change.

faq.question: How does it decide what can be edited?
faq.answer: It resolves the active bootstrap and governing spec chain, then requires clear write authority before edits.

faq.question: What happens if a request violates a spec?
faq.answer: The assistant should stop, explain the conflicting rule, and ask for corrected authority or a revised request.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
